page_meta_description: Zenith allocates your daily time budget across tasks using the Zenith Gradient algorithm — balancing effort, enjoyment, and flow state to maximize productivity.
header_tagline: Splits your daily time budget so each extra minute goes to the task where it counts most — accounting for flow ramp-up, context-switch costs, and your cognitive and physical capacity, and never pushing a task past its point of diminishing returns.
list_empty_hint: Add a task above to begin tracking
task_run_order_tooltip: Suggested run order — alternates cognitive and physical work so the resting energy system recovers
task_nature_cognitive_description: Cognitive task — mental difficulty dominates, draws on the cognitive capacity pool
task_nature_physical_description: Physical task — physical difficulty dominates, draws on the physical capacity pool
task_nature_hybrid_description: Hybrid task — balanced mental and physical demands, draws on both capacity pools
task_inputs_tooltip: Your slider inputs: Physical difficulty, Mental difficulty, Enjoyment — hover the task and use ✎ to change them
task_derived_tooltip: What the Zenith model derived from your sliders: true effort E (1–5), time to reach flow state ϕ, and the optimal stopping time (1.79×ϕ) beyond which average productivity declines
task_flow_badge_tooltip: Measured minutes-to-flow you logged for this task — data points like this personalize the model's c₁,c₂,c₃ constants (least-squares fit)
task_allocation_tooltip: Suggested time allocation for today, and the task's priority score (intrinsic value × 10) that drives it
task_log_flow_tooltip: Log how many minutes it took to get into flow — measured data points personalize the model's c₁,c₂,c₃ constants
budget_unplanned: {hours}h left unplanned — capped by optimal stopping times & capacity pools
budget_unplanned_title: The plan deliberately stops short of your budget: each task is capped at its optimal stopping time (1.79×ϕ, where average productivity peaks) and by your cognitive/physical capacity pools. The unused hours are recovery time, not lost time.
budget_cognitive_hint: Intense mental work you sustain per day (~4h)
budget_physical_hint: Intense physical work you sustain per day (~6h)
budget_model_tooltip: The model's flow-state constants (c₁, c₂, c₃) are a ridge least-squares fit of your measured time-to-flow data, anchored to the article's defaults. Click to manage the logged data points.
budget_delete_log_title: Remove this data point — the model refits without it
budget_reset_confirm: Delete all {count} logs and revert to defaults?
budget_reset_title: Delete all flow logs — the constants are always derived from the logs (never stored), so the model reverts to the article defaults
momentum_tooltip: Average enjoyment minus difficulty. Upward = sustainable pace, Reset Reqd = burnout risk.
model_status_personalized: Model personalized from {count} time-to-flow logs (⚡) — blended with the defaults, sharpens as you log more
model_status_personalized_one: Model personalized from 1 time-to-flow log (⚡) — blended with the defaults, sharpens as you log more
model_status_implausible: Your {count} flow logs produced an implausible fit (predicted flow times over 16h) — using default constants. Check the logs below for mistakes.
model_status_implausible_one: Your 1 flow log produced an implausible fit (predicted flow times over 16h) — using default constants. Check the logs below for mistakes.
model_status_default: Model uses default constants — log time-to-flow (⚡) on tasks to start personalizing
banner_future_body: tasks and budget you set here are saved to {date} — open it on that day to work the plan.
banner_past_body: you can still check tasks on or off (forgot one before midnight? fix it here — changes are saved), but adding or editing tasks is only possible on today.
metric_zenith_gain_desc: Productivity improvement from Zenith optimization vs. naive equal time split. Based on the Lagrange multiplier solution.
metric_yield_index_desc: Did you complete the highest-priority tasks? Compares your completed work against the optimal selection.
metric_completion_rate_desc: Priority-weighted progress. High-value tasks contribute more to this percentage than low-priority ones.
metric_flow_coverage_desc: Tasks that reach flow state (allocated time ≥ ϕ). Low coverage means too many tasks for budget — drop tasks or add hours.
metric_human_capacity_desc: {type} limit ({hours}h/day, configurable in Time Budget). The allocator enforces these pools, so suggested plans stay ≤100% — this shows how much of your sustainable capacity the plan uses.
metric_time_scarcity_desc: How stretched is your time budget? Higher values mean too many tasks for the hours available.
metric_bottleneck_desc: The task requiring the most energy to reach flow state (highest effort-to-enjoyability ratio in Zenith terms).
metric_burnout_risk_desc: Strain accumulates when difficulty > enjoyment (weighted by mental intensity), plus budget overhang: hours you plan beyond the optimal workload (1.79×ϕ per task) land in the diminishing-returns zone and count double. 5 strain-hours = 100% risk.
metric_cognitive_load_desc: Percentage of your day allocated to mental/cognitive tasks.
metric_physical_load_desc: Percentage of your day allocated to physical tasks.
metric_energy_balance_desc: Distribution of Cognitive vs physical work. Alternating types extends total productive hours.
metric_schedule_integrity_desc: Detects fragmentation. Drops if tasks get less than 15 minutes, or if no time budget is set.
metric_friction_index_desc: Share of your allocated time spent on high-difficulty, low-enjoyment work (time-weighted average of E−β). 100% = every planned hour is maximum-friction work.
metric_deep_work_desc: Percentage of time allocated to high mental difficulty (≥7) tasks requiring sustained focus.
metric_quick_wins_desc: Count of easy, enjoyable tasks available for momentum building and motivation boosts.
metric_task_variety_desc: Diversity across mental/physical spectrum. Mixing cognitive, physical, and balanced tasks prevents fatigue.
metric_grind_density_desc: Percentage of tasks where difficulty exceeds enjoyment. High values signal willpower drain.
metric_sustainable_work_desc: Percentage of time on tasks where enjoyment ≥ difficulty. Higher = more energizing workday.
metric_recovery_ratio_desc: Easy tasks (≤4) per hard tasks (≥7). Aim for 1:2 or 1:3 ratio for ultradian rhythm recovery.
metric_day_profile_desc: Your day's character based on average difficulty and enjoyment. Flow Zone = challenging and engaging, Grind Mode = demanding work, Cruise = light and enjoyable, Routine = low-key tasks.
metric_avg_physical_desc: Average physical difficulty across all tasks.
metric_avg_mental_desc: Average mental/cognitive difficulty across all tasks.
metric_avg_enjoyment_desc: Average enjoyment across all tasks. Higher values indicate more engaging work.
cal_meta_description: Month and week overview of your Zenith days.
cal_empty_1: No data in this {view} yet — days you plan on the
ana_meta_description: Completion trends and task stats across your Zenith days.
ana_subtitle: How your days have gone — completion, streaks, and day profiles over time.
ana_empty: Nothing to analyze in this range yet.
ana_empty_hint_2: page and it will show up here.
ana_with_completion: {count} with at least one task done
ana_streak_note: consecutive days with a completed task
ana_chart_hint_day: Priority-weighted completion rate per day — hover a bar for details
ana_chart_hint_year: Monthly average of the priority-weighted daily completion rate
ana_chart_aria: Bar chart of completion rate over the {range}
ana_day_profiles_hint: Character of your active days: challenging & engaging (Flow), demanding (Grind), light & fun (Cruise), or low-key (Routine)
energy_meta_description: Experimental total-output scheduler: energy reservoirs drain as you work and recover as you rest; breaks and quitting time emerge from the math.
energy_intro_1: A different engine than the main page: instead of maximizing average productivity, this scheduler maximizes
energy_intro_2: across your day. Two energy reservoirs (cognitive and physical) drain while you work and recover while you rest, and warm-up restarts every time you switch — so rest breaks, task order, and your quitting time all
energy_intro_3: instead of being rules. It shares the task list with the main page — edits here save to today's session.
energy_no_open_tasks_hint: Add one below — the Energy Lab schedules the same task list as the main page.
energy_set_window: Set a day window above 0 hours.
energy_chart_aria: Energy levels and output rate over the day
energy_drag_hint: Drag a slider and watch the schedule above re-optimize. Edits save to today's session.
energy_nothing_scheduled: Nothing scheduled — with the current parameters, free time is worth more than any task's output. Lower the free-time value or check your day window.
energy_vs_classic: Output vs the classic plan, judged by this model
energy_reset_defaults_title: Restore all model parameters to their defaults
energy_day_window_hint: Follows today's budget until you change it; changes stay in the lab
energy_cognitive_drain_hint: How fast deep mental work empties the tank
energy_recovery_rate_hint: How fast rest refills the reservoirs
energy_free_time_value_hint: What an hour NOT worked is worth to you, in output units — this is what makes the plan stop instead of filling the whole day
energy_evening_energy_hint: How much ending the day fresh (vs wrecked) is worth
energy_satiety_hint: Diminishing returns for piling more of the day onto the same task — after this many good sessions' worth of output, extra output on it counts half. 0 turns it off (one task can then win the whole day)
energy_micro_recovery_hint: Share of recovery that stays active even while working flat out (micro-pauses between efforts) — keeps a maximum-demand task above a small energy floor instead of draining toward zero. 0 turns it off
energy_log_drain_tooltip: After working on this task, rate how drained you feel (mind and body, 0–10) — the ratings calibrate the drain rates to you
energy_drain_mind_title: How mentally drained you feel now: 0 = fresh, 10 = completely spent
energy_drain_body_title: How physically drained you feel now: 0 = fresh, 10 = completely spent
energy_calibration_hint: Fits the two drain rates to your 🪫 end-of-session ratings (anchored to the defaults — every rating nudges them further). Recovery still comes from the sliders: only how fast work drains you is measurable from end-of-session ratings.
energy_calibration_empty: No ratings yet. After a session on a task, hit 🪫 on it and rate how drained your mind and body feel — a few ratings personalize the drain rates.
energy_apply_fit_title: Set the two drain-rate parameters to the fitted values
energy_delete_drain_log_title: Remove this rating from the calibration
energy_reset_drain_title: Delete every drain rating — the calibration reverts to the defaults
